trail.val: $99.4B, ~4x YoY; $40B+ new bookings; ~75% of 2027's >$30B guide contracted
Cantor models updated total RPO ~$125–130B; sees big RPO beat at 2Q (cons. $104B) + likely Anthropic deal

forward.note: Anchors the multi-year visibility; Cantor expects 2nd straight $30–40B TCV-add quarter.

trail.val: exit ARR raised to $18B–$19B; FY26 rev reaffirmed $12B–$13B
Cantor: ~$17B ARR end-FY25 → $30B FY26; 2027/28 ests up 15–20%; run-rate EBITDA ~$25–26B in '27 (vs $18B)

forward.note: Cantor sees 2026 as sold-out/irrelevant — the re-rate is in 2027/28 numbers.

trail.val: Bernstein: "nearly unhittable per math" — needs fit-out <8wks AND stabilized margins >21%
Bernstein reit. UP $67; steady-state margin guided 25–30% but reported "volatile and below steady-state for an extended period"

forward.note: Fit-out time + stabilized contribution margin are the key 2H26 bear bogeys.
Debt-funded build is the crux of the leverage/FCF bear case.

trail.val: 1+ GW active, 3.5+ GW contracted; on track for 1.7+ GW active by YE2026

forward.note: Power ramp gates the revenue conversion of backlog.

trail.val: Hedgeye: like-for-like neocloud GPU pricing fell for first time in 3 months — leading indicator of softer contract pricing
JPM: rebounding Neocloud H100 rental index = "draconian obsolescence assumptions flawed"; supportive of ~6yr schedule
Cantor: consensus moved 3–4yr → 7–8yr; thinks true life ~10yr; re-contracting holds as original taker renews

forward.note: GPU depreciation schedule is the crux of the reported-profitability bear case; sources diverge sharply.
